title: Micron Technology (MU) Stock Analysis: Riding the HBM Memory Boom
text: The analysis examines Micron's position in the high-bandwidth memory market. The company supplies HBM chips used in AI accelerators, with revenues split between DRAM and NAND products. Micron has sold out its 2025 HBM supply and is signing customers to long-term agreements that stretch well into 2026. Three main factors support the 2026 rally: AI Expansion - Hyperscalers continuously build training clusters requiring more HBM per accelerator. Supply Discipline - Memory manufacturers reduced capacity after 2022-2023 downturn, normalizing inventories. Product Mix Shift - HBM production consumes more wafer capacity per gigabit, tightening DDR5 supply. The stock had rallied approximately 68% year-to-date as of mid-2026. The analysis identifies four critical concerns: Memory cyclicality and inevitable price reversions, Intense competition from SK Hynix (market leader) and Samsung, Technology transition challenges with next-generation HBM generations, Geographic concentration in Taiwan and Korea for advanced packaging. The article recommends treating MU as cyclical rather than a buy-and-hold holding, emphasizing position sizing and disciplined trimming during market peaks.
